title: Li Auto Performance Plunges: BEV Transition Faces Headwinds
text: Li Auto's performance has faced significant challenges in its battery electric vehicle (BEV) transition. The Li MEGA has become the best-selling MPV priced above RMB 500,000 since May 2025, achieving approximately 3,000 units sold monthly, 14 months after launch in March 2024. It became the top-seller among all BEVs in the same price range since June 2025. However, the broader earnings picture deteriorated significantly. Orders for the Li i6 and Li i8 BEV models exceeded 100,000 units, but a 1.1 billion-RMB recall liability for the Li MEGA combined with lower production volumes, eroded profitability and increased per-unit manufacturing costs. Li Auto reported Q3 2025 revenue of RMB 27.4 billion and 93,211 vehicle deliveries, a 39.0% year-over-year decline in units. Vehicle margin was 15.5% in Q3 2025 compared to 20.9% in Q3 2024, but excluding the impact of estimated recall costs for the Li MEGA, vehicle margin would have been 19.8%. The company's transition from extended-range electric vehicles (EREVs) to pure BEVs is being challenged by intense market competition and the need to maintain profitability while scaling new platforms.
